paper_title: Glacier Dynamics and Modeling in the Cordillera Blanca

methods.method_name: Glacier Mass Balance Model: Cellular Automata
methods.description: This method models the glacier dynamics 160 years back to the Little Ice Age (LIA) using a cellular automata technique in MATLAB. It simulates glacier flow and dynamics based on landscape evolution and hydrologic water balance models.

steps.description: Model the glacier dynamics 160 years back to the LIA using a cellular automata technique in MATLAB.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate glacier dynamics using cellular automata in MATLAB
inputs: glacier boundary of the initial year
initial year of mass balance maximum gradient

steps.description: Verify the accuracy of the model by comparing the results of the test run with 1962 glacier polygons over both YAN and QUE.
steps.searchable_summary: validate model accuracy by comparing with historical glacier data

methods.description: This method sets up the cellular automata model by discretizing the landscape into a hexagonal lattice and defining the rules for glacier particle motion and mass balance.

steps.description: Discretize the landscape into a regular hexagonal lattice computed from 100-m resolution DEMs.
steps.searchable_summary: create hexagonal lattice from DEMs for model setup

steps.description: Set the ELA as the median glacier altitude for each glacier model run.
steps.searchable_summary: set ELA as median glacier altitude for model

steps.description: Define the rules for glacier particle motion and mass balance, including plasticity and yield stress.
steps.searchable_summary: define glacier particle motion and mass balance rules

methods.method_name: Volume and Surface Area (V-S) Scaling from the Model
methods.description: This method performs volume and surface area scaling for individual glaciers using a power law relationship to relate glacier volume to surface area.

steps.description: Perform V-S scaling for individual glaciers using a power law relationship with empirically computed scaling coefficients.
steps.searchable_summary: apply V-S scaling using power law relationship
outputs: scaled glacier volume and surface area
